description: Create a content slide on AI adoption in risk management with a two-column layout featuring two horizontal bar charts and a bottom summary panel of research highlights.

header.headline: AI adoption in risk management
header.sub: Within risk management operations, chatbots (67%) and detection of digital fraud and financial crime (61%) are the most common initial use cases. AI tools for cyber and operational risk management (41%), credit and market risk modeling (33%) and legal and document analysis (30%) are also in place at significant percentages of banks.

left.title: Figure 10: Current state of AI adoption in risk management

series.label: Limited adoption – strategy, governance framework and policy being implemented, use cases have been identified, some adoption
Planning but no adoption – strategy, governance framework and policy being developed, use cases identified, but no adoption
Benefits applied – strategy, governance framework and policy implemented, use cases implemented, benefits applied
No strategy – have not set strategy or policy (i.e., starting to think about it)

labels: Limited adoption – strategy, governance framework and policy being implemented, use cases have been identified, some adoption
Planning but no adoption – strategy, governance framework and policy being developed, use cases identified, but no adoption
Benefits applied – strategy, governance framework and policy implemented, use cases implemented, benefits applied
No strategy – have not set strategy or policy (i.e., starting to think about it)

right.title: Figure 11: Most significant areas of AI use in risk management

series.label: General use of chatbots (e.g., LLM integration with certain applications)
Digital fraud and financial crime detection (e.g., AML, transaction monitoring, pattern recognition)
Cyber and operational risk management (e.g., anomaly detection, network surveillance, control testing automation)
Credit and market risk modeling (e.g., credit assessments, volatility forecasting, portfolio risk)
Legal and document analysis (e.g., contract review, documentation automation)
Model risk management (e.g., model performance, documentation, code migration)

items.text: The latest IIF-EY Annual Survey Report on AI Use in Financial Services shows how banks are adopting this revolutionary technology across all parts of the business, complementing our annual risk management survey. It's clear that deployments in different functions are outpacing those in risk management.
100% of institutions planning changes in their AI investments are looking to increase them, with 36% of G-SIBs planning increases of 100% or more.
83% reported some level of work on agentic AI – 23% are in production, 34% are piloting and 26% are still planning.
79% of all institutions and 100% of G-SIBs said data quality was the top challenge for AI development, with data availability a very close second.
77% use third-party platforms to address challenges (e.g., data preparation, access to training data and AI talent) and bring AI into production.
